steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The sentence pairs a positive fact (frequent citations) with a positive consequence (usefulness of research). The dash-elaboration confirms: “other economists clearly find her studies valuable.” The blank must express a positive relationship: that citations demonstrate or reveal usefulness.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: Underscores means to emphasize or highlight importance—exactly what frequent citations do in demonstrating research value. The other choices contradict this required positive relationship: forestalls prevents usefulness, overshadows diminishes importance, and belies contradicts or misleads. That matches choice D — D is correct.
